reforms.description: The government has implemented several initiatives to improve its innovation performance, including establishing core funding streams for Crown Research Institutes and aiming to double business R&D spending to 1% of GDP.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research institutes is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has established core funding streams for research institutes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding landscape for research, aiming to enhance the overall innovation ecosystem in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The government has implemented several initiatives to improve its innovation performance consistent with recommendations from the 2007 OECD Review of Innovation Policy.

reforms.description: The government introduced Technology Development Grants to reimburse 20% of eligible expenditures for established technology-intensive firms with a strong R&D history, aiming to support innovation despite some administrative complexities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to technology-intensive firms, the grants aim to stimulate R&D activities and enhance innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Technology Development Grants program was established to support technology-intensive firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the program is significant, it is a modification of existing support mechanisms rather than a complete overhaul of the innovation funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: An R&D tax credit was introduced in 2009 but was quickly repealed and replaced with a modest programme of Technology Development Grants.

reforms.description: The government established Callaghan Innovation, a Crown agency aimed at improving the transfer of knowledge, know-how, and technology to firms, while connecting businesses accessing grants with other publicly funded R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the ability of firms to innovate by facilitating access to research and technology, thereby improving productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: Callaghan Innovation has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of Callaghan Innovation represents a significant structural change in how New Zealand supports innovation and business development, with potential long-term impacts on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has established Callaghan Innovation to improve the transfer of knowledge, know-how and technology to firms.

reforms.description: The government is promoting research and innovation in the primary sector through significant co-investments with industry, particularly in high-value manufacturing and services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By targeting investments in sectors with high potential for productivity gains, this initiative aims to enhance economic growth and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively co-investing with industry to promote research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a major commitment to enhancing the productivity of the primary sector, which is crucial for New Zealand's economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is moving in this direction through significant co-investments with industry to promote research and innovation in the primary sector.

reforms.description: The government plans to redesign the Technology Development Grants to clarify and simplify the approval criteria, ensuring better access for small start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying access to funding, the reform aims to enhance innovation and support the growth of small businesses, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to redesign the Technology Development Grants.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to an existing funding mechanism, aimed at improving access for small start-ups, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Redesign the Technology Development Grants to clarify and simplify the approval criteria and ensure access for small start-ups.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government is seeking ways to boost trade intensity, aiming to increase the share of exports to 40% of GDP by 2025, which is expected to enhance international connections and knowledge transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing trade intensity is projected to raise annual real output growth by facilitating faster diffusion of technology and knowledge from abroad.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set an ambitious goal to increase the share of exports to 40% of GDP by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing New Zealand's international trade and competitiveness, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is seeking ways to boost trade intensity and has set an ambitious goal within its Business Growth Agenda to increase the share of exports to 40% of GDP by 2025.

reforms.description: New Zealand aims to facilitate services trade through the development of high-performance ICT infrastructure, which is expected to drive future growth by enabling smaller firms to enter foreign markets at lower costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving ICT infrastructure is likely to enhance the capacity for services trade, which employs high-skilled workers and contributes to higher wages.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is focusing on expanding the role of services trade through high-performance ICT infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets the enhancement of infrastructure critical for enabling services trade, which is a growing sector in New Zealand's economy.
reforms.source_quote: Facilitate services trade through high-performance ICT infrastructure.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government is investing significantly in laying ultra-fast broadband (UFB) to provide higher speed Internet access to 75% of the population by 2020, involving NZD1.5 billion in direct subsidies through public-private partnerships.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband infrastructure is expected to enhance productivity and facilitate better connectivity for businesses, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently investing significantly in laying ultra-fast broadband.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a major investment in national infrastructure that is expected to have long-term positive effects on productivity and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently investing significantly in laying ultra-fast broadband (UFB) with the goal of bringing higher speed Internet access to 75% of the population by 2020.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has introduced funding to establish two to four National Science Challenges aimed at strengthening the linkages between science and business and improving commercialisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between science and industry, this initiative is expected to facilitate the transfer of knowledge and technology, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government introduced funding to establish two to four National Science Challenges.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to improve the commercialisation of research, which is crucial for enhancing innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced funding to establish two to four National Science Challenges to strengthen the linkages between science and business and improve commercialisation.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has introduced Technology Development Grants (TDGs) to support technology-intensive firms, providing NZD60 million annually to fund business R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The grants aim to enhance business R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The grants have been operational since their introduction in 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The TDGs represent a significant shift in funding support for R&D, replacing the previous R&D tax credit and targeting technology-intensive firms.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced Technology Development Grants (TDGs) to support technology-intensive firms.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government plans to increase tuition subsidy rates for engineering students, aiming to raise the number of engineering graduates by 500 per year by 2017.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of engineering graduates is expected to address skill shortages in the sector, thereby enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to increase tuition subsidies and graduate numbers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for addressing engineering skill shortages, which are crucial for innovation in technology sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced it will provide NZD42 million over four years to increase tuition subsidy rates for engineering students.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government is shifting the allocation of the NZ Venture Investment Fund to provide greater support for early-expansion stage firms, addressing equity financing gaps.
reforms.component_name: NZ Venture Investment Fund Allocation Shift
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing support for early-stage firms, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to increased economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively shifted the allocation of the fund to support early-expansion stage firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: To address equity financing gaps, shift the allocation of the NZ Venture Investment Fund to provide greater support for early-expansion stage firms.

reforms.description: The government recently announced industry training reforms that remove the modern apprenticeship and create a single apprenticeship programme for youth and adults under the 'New Zealand apprenticeship', which boosts funding and sets clear performance criteria for Industry Training Organisations (ITOs).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing apprenticeship funding and performance criteria, the reform aims to improve skill supply and match training with labor market needs, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government recently announced industry training reforms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the apprenticeship system, aiming to improve the quality and accessibility of vocational training, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently announced industry training reforms that remove the modern apprenticeship and create a single apprenticeship programme for youth and adults.
